results.name: Operation BlockBuster unveils the actors behind the Sony attacks
results.description:
Today, a coordinated coalition involving AlienVault and several other security companies led by Novetta is announcing Operation BlockBuster. This industry initiative was created to share information and potentially disrupt the infrastructure and tools from an actor named the Lazarus Group. The Lazarus Group has been responsible for several operations since at least 2009, including the attack that affected Sony Pictures Entertainment in 2014.

We are not including the IP addresses from the C2 infrastructure since it is compromised infrastructure that is not longer in use

results.name: Lazarus group: Operation Blockbuster
results.description:
The Lazarus Group’s activity spans multiple years, going back as far as 2009. However, their activity spikes starting with 2011. The group deployed multiple malware families throughout the years, including malware associated with Operation Troy and DarkSeoul, the Hangman malware (2014-2015) and Wild Positron / Duuzer (2015). The group is known for spearphishing attacks, which include CVE-2015-6585, which was a zero-day vulnerability at the time of discovery.

During our analysis of the malware from the SPE attack as well as the connected malware families mentioned above, we observed certain specific traits shared between samples used in separate attacks. In general, such similarities are instances of code sharing and indicate the existence of a relationship between the malware families, which can be used to paint a more complete picture of a threat actor. We describe some of these overlapping features below.

Source: https://securelist.com/blog/incidents/73914/operation-blockbuster-revealed/

results.name: Operation Blockbuster: The Lazarus Group
results.description: Operation Blockbuster is a Novetta led coalition that analyzed the Advanced Persistent Threat adversary responsible for the devastating and destructive attack against Sony Pictures in 2014. The threat has been dubbed the Lazarus Group , and is tied to use of specific similar malicious code variants going back to 2009. The actor has been identified in attacks on government , Military , Media , Aerospace , Finance , and Critical Infrastructure victims, most of which are in the United States and South Korea . The scope of this group indicates that it is operated by a well-funded and resourced entity, not merely a hacktivist group.

results.name: The Blockbuster Sequel
results.description: Unit 42 has identified malware with recent compilation and distribution timestamps that has code, infrastructure, and themes overlapping with threats described previously in the Operation Blockbuster report, written by researchers at Novetta. This report details the activities from a group they named Lazarus, their tools, and the techniques they use to infiltrate computer networks. The Lazarus group is tied to the 2014 attack on Sony Pictures Entertainment and the 2013 DarkSeoul attacks.

results.name: WannaCry - Lazarus Group
results.description: Similarities in code and infrastructure indicate close connection to group that was linked to Sony Pictures and Bangladesh Bank attacks

results.name: WannaCry: Ransomware attacks show strong links to Lazarus group
results.description: May 22 2017 - Updated bulletin with traits and artifacts supporting attribution  to the Lazarus group.

results.name: Lazarus Arisen
results.description: IOCs from Group-IB Lazarus Arisen Report

results.name: From BlackEnergy to ExPetr - Securelist
results.description:
Much has been written about the recent ExPetr/NotPetya/Nyetya/Petya outbreak – you can read our findings here:Schroedinger’s Pet(ya) and ExPetr is a wiper, not ransomware.

As in the case of Wannacry, attribution is very difficult and finding links with previously known malware is challenging. In the case of Wannacry, Google’s Neel Mehta was able to identify a code fragment which became the most important clue in the story, and was later confirmed by further evidence, showing Wannacry as a pet project of the Lazarus group.

results.name: Report on North Korean cyber attacks (Campaign Rifle)
results.description: In February 2016, Novetta announced a profiling report entitled &quot;Operation Blockbuster: Unraveling the Long Thread of Sony Attack&quot; in association with global security companies (Kaspersky Lab, Symantec, Trend Micro, JPCERT / CC, etc.). The Lazarus group, which has been identified as the backbone of the report, has been active in the past, and Novetta &#39;s research is helping to preemptively counteract and prevent Lazarus attacks around the world. However, global security companies are limited in collecting attack information in Korea, and there is also a lack of information about the attacks that Lazarus or Lazarus are suspected of as a small group of threat groups in Korea. Therefore, the Korea Financial Security Institute (FSI) identified the specific characteristics of domestic IT and work environment, and profiled specific threat groups that are using the attacks. The results are described in this report.
